differential_diagnoses: Thoracic aortic vasculitis or periaortitis (possibly infectious/mycotic aneurysm)
Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia with metastatic foci (e.g., spondylodiscitis, sternoclavicular joint)
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography to rule out infective endocarditis
Order urgent CT angiography of the chest and abdomen with focus on the thoracic aorta to evaluate for mycotic aneurysm or dissection
Obtain MRI of the thoracic spine to rule out spondylodiscitis or epidural abscess
Continue targeted intravenous antibiotics for Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia
Consult Infectious Disease, Cardiology, and Vascular Surgery teams
Monitor blood pressure closely and consider imaging for the retrocardiac opacity identified on chest X-ray
